claim: Harness engineering is about building the AI layer around a coding model with rules, skills, hooks, and sub-agents—98% of Claude Code is the harness, not the model
actionable: Focus on building and improving your harness (rules, hooks, skills) over waiting for better models
source_title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now

claim: System evolution treats every agent failure as an opportunity to improve the harness: each mistake becomes a rule or hook that prevents recurrence
actionable: When an agent fails, add a rule to agents.md, a hook to prevent destructive commands, or a skill to improve behavior for next session
source_title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now

claim: Planning, implementation, and validation should each run in separate agent sessions to keep tokens focused and manageable
actionable: Create three separate skills (plan, implement, validate) and run them in sequential agent sessions with artifact handoffs
source_title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now

claim: Pre-tool-use hooks can block destructive commands (like 'rm -rf'); stop-validation hooks force agents to iterate until all tests pass
actionable: Use hooks to prevent reading .env files, destructive deletions, and to enforce test-passing before marking implementation complete
source_title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now

claim: The Ralph loop orchestrates multiple coding agent sessions automatically by splitting large PRDs into focused tasks, each handled by a separate agent
actionable: Build a Ralph-style orchestrator that runs plan, implement, and review agents in sequence with artifact handoffs
source_title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now

claim: Running multiple review agents in parallel (security, correctness, simplicity) and iterating on implementation until all pass improves code quality
actionable: Set up review agents for different aspects (security, design correctness, code simplicity) to run after implementation
source_title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now

claim: Global rules in agents.md define patterns and constraints; skills define workflows (plan, implement, validate); hooks provide security and validation
actionable: Organize your harness into rules (constraints), skills (workflows), and hooks (security/validation) for clarity and maintainability
source_title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now
